name: Midway Islands (United States)

colors.symbolism: the upper sky over the atoll
the central band representing the horizon and the surf at the reef
an albatross in flight, representing the famous Laysan albatrosses ('gooney birds') of Midway

description: Three horizontal bands: a tall sky-blue band at the top, a narrow white band, and a lighter blue band along the bottom. A brown Laysan albatross is shown in flight across the centre of the field, with the white band suggesting the horizon and the foam of the reef.
history: Midway Islands, a US Minor Outlying Island and National Wildlife Refuge in the central Pacific, used this unofficial flag during the late 20th century while the islands were operated as a US Naval Air Facility and afterward as a wildlife refuge. The flag was flown locally rather than formally adopted by Congress. The ISO 3166-3 code MIUM records the territory's former ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 code 'MI' (1974-1986), withdrawn when the islands were merged into the broader 'UM' (United States Minor Outlying Islands) entry.

features.symbolism: Sky blue above the white horizon line of the central band, ocean blue below.
the most iconic wildlife of the atoll, which hosts the world's largest colony of Laysan albatrosses
